symptom: an achy stiff arm with hard tender bumps under the skin that sometimes leaked a chalky white paste
hook: the whole arm was filling up with hundreds of bright white stones scattered through the soft tissue
whatYouSee: The skin and muscle around the arm are stuffed with hundreds of bright white specks lumps and sheets like someone poured gravel and crushed chalk under the skin. The bones look ordinary so the white storm sitting outside them is clearly in the wrong place.
whyItMatters: This is the body laying down real calcium in the soft tissue where none belongs usually driven by an autoimmune muscle disease. The deposits can break the skin lock joints and get infected.
treatment: Doctors treat the underlying autoimmune disease to calm the inflammation and sometimes remove stubborn painful or infected lumps surgically.
takeaway: When the soft tissue around a limb is packed with hundreds of chalky deposits while the bones stay normal the body is making calcium in all the wrong places.
igOptions: A. Calcium building up in the soft tissue
C. Shotgun pellets from an old injury

generated.threadsCaption:
A patient came in with an achy stiff arm with hard tender bumps under the skin that sometimes leaked a chalky white paste.

Then the X-ray loaded 😭

And the whole arm was filling up with hundreds of bright white stones scattered through the soft tissue.

Quick diagnosis challenge 🩻

What's the most likely diagnosis?

Wild guesses are welcome 👀
generated.threadsAnswer:
Answer: Calcinosis universalis

👀 What you see:
The skin and muscle around the arm are stuffed with hundreds of bright white specks lumps and sheets like someone poured gravel and crushed chalk under the skin. The bones look ordinary so the white storm sitting outside them is clearly in the wrong place.

💊 Treatment:
Doctors treat the underlying autoimmune disease to calm the inflammation and sometimes remove stubborn painful or infected lumps surgically.
